theme: dynamic pricing of perishable event tickets
title: Last Curtain Call: Dynamic Ticket Pricing
strategies: flat frozen discount: read only the day-one demand curve, undercut it to guarantee a sellout, hold that single price the whole event (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference; blind to drift, anchoring, and stock)

per-period myopic monopolist: repost the revenue-maximizing price for each period's live demand curve; adapts to the changing schedule but ignores remaining stock and remaining time, so it depletes inventory too fast on back-loaded or scarce instances and leaves a richer late crowd unserved

inventory-pacing / shadow-value proxy: solve each period's demand curve for the price that sells at the rate remaining_stock / remaining_periods, letting scarcity relative to time-left pull price up (ration) and abundance relative to time-left pull price down (clear stock before it perishes)
exact dynamic programming / model-predictive re-optimization over the full known schedule, or learned corrections to the pacing target, to push closer to the never-reached ceiling
